notes.title: Abstract Nouns
notes.text: Works best with abstract nouns representing goals: 취업, 연구, 학습, 개발, 판매, 홍보, 규명.

notes.title: 하다 / 삼다
notes.text: When used as a predicate, 목적으로 하다 or 목적으로 삼다 nominalizes the purpose. E.g., 연구를 목적으로 한다.

explanation: A compound verb structure marking the task or matter being assisted with as the direct object. While 도와주다 is common, the formal object-marking pattern N-을/를 도와주다 is a key collocational grammar point at the advanced level, especially when distinguishing the task from the beneficiary (marked with -에게/한테).

rules.title: Helping with a specific task
rules.description: Mark the task or matter with 을/를 and follow with 도와주다. The person being helped is often marked separately with -에게/한테.

examples.breakdown: 이사 (move) + 를 도와주었다 (helped with) → helped with the move
examples.english: A friend helped with the move.

examples.breakdown: 장보기 (grocery shopping) + 를 도와주었다 (helped with) → helped with grocery shopping
examples.english: My younger sibling helped with the grocery shopping.

rules.title: Honorific and humble forms
rules.description: At advanced levels, the helper's status and the speaker's position determine whether to use 도와주시다, 도와드리다, or other elevated forms.

examples.breakdown: 숙제 (homework) + 를 도와주셨다 (honorific: helped with) → the teacher helped with the homework
examples.english: The teacher helped with the homework.

notes.title: Object-Marking Pattern
notes.text: This advanced pattern marks the task or matter being helped with as the direct object. The person helped is often marked with -에게/한테.

notes.title: Honorific Usage
notes.text: Use 도와주시다 or 도와드리다 to elevate the helper. 도와드리다 is humble when the speaker is the helper.

notes.title: Alternatives
notes.text: For formal or written contexts, N-을/를 거들다 or N-을/를 조력하다 (Sino-Korean) can be used, though they are much rarer.

explanation: Means 'centered on.' It can describe physical centrality (spatial) or leadership and organizational centrality (figurative). It is a standard advanced pattern in formal writing, geography, economics, and sociology. It implies that everything else revolves around the marked noun.

rules.title: Spatial and figurative centrality
rules.description: Attach 을/를 중심으로 to a noun representing a place, group, or person. The following clause describes the arrangement or activity revolving around it.

examples.breakdown: 서울 (Seoul) + 을 중심으로 (centered on) → centered on Seoul
examples.english: The metropolitan area is formed, centered on Seoul.

examples.korean: 이 프로젝트는 그를 중심으로 진행되고 있다.
examples.breakdown: 그를 (him) + 중심으로 (centered on) → centered on him
examples.english: This project is proceeding, centered on him.

rules.title: 하여 / 잡고 variants
rules.description: Expand with 하여 or 잡고 to create more complex sentences where the focus is intentional or strategic.

examples.korean: 학생들을 중심으로 하여 운 동이 전개되었다.
examples.breakdown: 학생들 (students) + 을 중심으로 하여 (centering on) → centering on students
examples.english: A movement unfolded, centered on students.

notes.title: Spatial and Figurative
notes.text: Can describe physical centrality (spatial) or leadership/organizational centrality (figurative). Both are common in advanced Korean.

notes.title: Common Nouns
notes.text: Frequently paired with 도시, 지역, 인물, 그룹, 가족, 회사, 학교, and 시장.

notes.title: 하여 / 잡고
notes.text: N-을/를 중심으로 하여 and N-을/를 중심으로 잡고 are valid variants that add nuance of intentional focus.

explanation: Derived from the Sino-Korean 重要視하다 (to regard as important), this is a formal, advanced alternative to 중요하게 생각하다 or 소중히 여기다. It elevates the expression and is common in editorials, essays, and formal speeches where the speaker wishes to stress the prioritization of an abstract value or principle.

rules.title: Valuing an abstract quality
rules.description: Attach 을/를 중요시하다 to a noun representing a value, principle, or quality. It functions as a main predicate or a modifier.

examples.breakdown: 직원 복지 (employee welfare) + 를 중요시한다 (values) → values employee welfare
examples.english: That company values employee welfare.

examples.breakdown: 예의 (courtesy) + 를 중요시하는 (valuing) → tendency to value courtesy
examples.english: Koreans tend to value courtesy.

rules.title: Formal written usage
rules.description: In academic and official contexts, it often modifies clauses or appears in imperative or hortative forms.

examples.breakdown: 창의력 (creativity) + 을 중요시해야 한다 (should prioritize) → should prioritize creativity
examples.english: In the educational field, creativity should be prioritized.

notes.title: Sino-Korean Verb
notes.text: Derived from 重要視하다 (to regard as important). It is a formal, advanced alternative to 중요하게 생각하다 or 소중히 여기다.

notes.title: Abstract Values
notes.text: Common objects include 가치, 전통, 신뢰, 예의, 시간, 안전, 환경, and 교육.

notes.title: Similar Expressions
notes.text: 중요시여기다 and 중요하게 다루다 are near-synonyms. 중요시하다 is the most common in formal writing.

explanation: Means 'to ignore' or 'to disregard.' It is an advanced Sino-Korean verb (無視하다) that carries a firm, critical tone. It is used when someone deliberately pays no attention to rules, opinions, warnings, or realities, often leading to negative consequences. Common in formal criticism, news, and academic writing.

rules.title: Ignoring a person, rule, or warning
rules.description: Attach 을/를 무시하다 to a noun representing something that should be heeded. The following clause often describes a resulting conflict or failure.

examples.korean: 그는 상대방의 의견을 무시하고 자기 주장만 펼쳤다.
examples.breakdown: 의견 (opinion) + 을 무시하고 (ignoring) → ignoring the other person's opinion
examples.english: He ignored the other person's opinion and only pushed his own claim.

examples.breakdown: 안전 수칙 (safety rules) + 을 무시해서 (ignoring) → because safety rules were ignored
examples.english: An accident occurred because safety rules were ignored.

rules.title: With adverbial and negative forms
rules.description: Can be used in imperative or negative constructions to warn against disregard.

examples.breakdown: 조언 (advice) + 을 무시하지 마라 (don't ignore) → don't ignore his advice
examples.english: Don't ignore his advice.

notes.title: Negative Consequences
notes.text: Often appears in contexts explaining cause and effect: ignoring something leads to accidents, conflicts, or failures.

notes.title: Common Objects
notes.text: Frequently paired with 의견, 규칙, 법, 위험, 신호, 조언, 현실, and 상대방.

notes.title: Formal Nuance
notes.text: In very formal writing, 무시하다 can be replaced with 경시하다 (to look down upon / neglect) for an even more elevated tone.

explanation: Means 'to emphasize.' It is the standard advanced verb (强調하다) for stressing the importance, necessity, or distinctiveness of something in speeches, academic writing, essays, and news. It is more forceful and formal than simple repetition or casual pointing out.

rules.title: Emphasizing a point
rules.description: Attach 을/를 강조하다 to an abstract noun representing a key message. It functions as the main predicate of the sentence.

examples.breakdown: 실용성 (practicality) + 을 강조하셨다 (emphasized) → emphasized the practicality
examples.english: The professor emphasized the practicality of the theory.

examples.breakdown: 필요성 (necessity) + 을 강조했다 (emphasized) → emphasized the necessity
examples.english: The president emphasized the necessity of economic recovery.

rules.title: With modifiers and embedded clauses
rules.description: At the advanced level, it frequently appears in relative clauses or with modifiers expressing the speaker's intent.

examples.breakdown: 점 (point) + 을 강조하고 싶은 (want to emphasize) → the point I want to emphasize
examples.english: The point I want to emphasize is the importance of responsibility.

notes.title: Formal Speech Pattern
notes.text: Standard in speeches, essays, academic writing, and news. It elevates the act of emphasizing beyond simple repetition.

notes.title: Common Objects
notes.text: Often used with 중요성, 필요성, 가치, 의미, 책임, 협력, 안전, and 차별성.

notes.title: Synonyms
notes.text: 강조하다 is more forceful than 부각하다 (highlight). For softer emphasis, consider 언급하다 (mention) or 짚다 (point out casually).

explanation: Means 'to point out.' It implies a deliberate, often expert analysis that identifies flaws, errors, problems, or contradictions. It carries an air of authority and is common in reviews, audits, academic critiques, and formal feedback. It is more analytical than 비판하다 (criticize) and more pointed than 언급하다 (mention).

rules.title: Pointing out a flaw or problem
rules.description: Attach 을/를 지적하다 to a noun representing a problem, error, or shortcoming. The subject is usually an authority or expert.

examples.breakdown: 결함 (flaws) + 을 지적했다 (pointed out) → pointed out logical flaws
examples.english: The judge pointed out logical flaws in the thesis.

examples.breakdown: 문제점 (problems) + 을 지적하였다 (pointed out) → pointed out the problems
examples.english: He pointed out the problems with the government policy.

rules.title: Used in formal criticism
rules.description: In literary, political, or social commentary, 지적하다 is the standard verb for identifying specific weaknesses.

examples.breakdown: 결말 (ending) + 을 지적했다 (pointed out) → pointed out the ending
examples.english: Critics pointed out the ending of the work.

notes.title: Critical Observation
notes.text: Implies a deliberate, often expert analysis that identifies flaws, errors, or problems. Common in reviews, audits, and critiques.

notes.title: Common Objects
notes.text: Frequently paired with 문제점, 오류, 결함, 부족함, 잘못, 모순, and 한계.

notes.title: Tone
notes.text: While not necessarily hostile, 지적하다 carries an air of authority. Use 비판하다 for stronger criticism or 언급하다 for neutral mention.

explanation: Means 'to mention' or 'to touch upon.' It describes bringing up a subject briefly without necessarily discussing it in depth. It is the standard formal verb (言及하다) for reference in reports, speeches, and academic writing. The connotation is neutral and often implies brevity.

rules.title: Mentioning briefly
rules.description: Attach 을/를 언급하다 to a noun representing a topic, fact, or name. The following clause usually indicates the manner or decision regarding that mention.

examples.breakdown: 과거의 일 (past events) + 을 언급하지 않았다 (did not mention) → did not mention past events
examples.english: He did not mention past events.

examples.korean: 보고서에서 이 문제를 언급할 필요가 있다.
examples.breakdown: 이 문제 (this issue) + 를 언급할 필요가 있다 (need to mention) → there is a need to mention this issue
examples.english: There is a need to mention this issue in the report.

rules.title: Avoiding mention
rules.description: The negative form is extremely common in formal contexts where discretion or strategy requires silence on a topic.

examples.breakdown: 그 사건 (that incident) + 을 언급하지 않기로 하자 (let's not mention) → let's decide not to mention
examples.english: Let's decide not to mention that incident.

notes.title: Brief Reference
notes.text: Means to mention or touch upon a subject briefly. It does not imply deep discussion, only that the topic was brought up.

notes.title: Common Objects
notes.text: Often used with 사실, 문제, 사건, 인물, 이름, 예, and 통계.

notes.title: Negative Forms
notes.text: 언급하지 않다, 언급하지 말다, and 언급을 피하다 are very common when deliberately avoiding a topic.

explanation: Means 'to include.' It is the standard formal verb (包含하다) for inclusion in mathematics, statistics, contracts, travel packages, and official documents. It explicitly states that one item is part of a larger whole or total.

rules.title: Including something
rules.description: Attach 을/를 포함하다 to a noun representing the item being included. It can function as a predicate or connect to the next clause with the -아/어 form.

examples.breakdown: 부록 (appendix) + 을 포함하여 (including) → including the appendix
examples.english: This book is 500 pages total, including the appendix.
The participation fee includes meals.

rules.title: Calculative inclusion
rules.description: In statistical or contractual contexts, it is often followed by 하여 or 하면 to specify how the inclusion affects the total.

examples.english: Everyone, including 10 people, attended—50 in total.

notes.title: Inclusion
notes.text: Used when one thing is part of a larger whole. It is the standard formal verb for inclusion in mathematics, statistics, and contracts.

notes.title: Common Objects
notes.text: Frequently paired with 사람, 비용, 세금, 부록, 시간, 점심, and 교통비.

notes.title: 하여 / 하면
notes.text: N-을/를 포함하여 (including) adds items to a total. N-을/를 포함하면 (if including) is used for conditional calculations.

explanation: Means 'to exclude.' It is the direct formal antonym of 포함하다 (包含하다). Used to explicitly remove something from a set, list, calculation, or scope. It is standard in contracts, schedules, statistics, and official notices.

rules.title: Excluding something
rules.description: Attach 을/를 제외하다 to a noun representing the item being excluded. The -고 or -으면/면 forms are the most common connectors.

examples.english: It operates every day, excluding weekends.
Everyone except him agreed.

rules.title: Conditional exclusion
rules.description: Use 제외하면 to frame the exclusion as a condition under which a statement holds true.

examples.english: Excluding costs, the net profit is 100 million won.

notes.title: Exclusion
notes.text: The direct opposite of 포함하다. Used to explicitly remove something from a set, list, or calculation.

notes.title: Common Objects
notes.text: Often paired with 주말, 공휴일, 세금, 비용, 그, 이 항목, and 부록.

notes.title: 하고 / 하면
notes.text: N-을/를 제외하고 (excluding) is the most common form. N-을/를 제외하면 (if excluding) frames the exclusion as a condition.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to form a formal verb meaning 'to consider' or 'to take into consideration.' This is a high-register expression typical of academic, business, and official contexts where careful evaluation of factors is required before reaching a decision.

rules.title: Basic Usage
rules.description: Attach 를/을 to a noun and follow with 고려하다 to indicate that the noun is being evaluated or factored into a decision.

examples.korean: 환경을 고려하여 새 정책을 수립해야 한다.
examples.breakdown: 환경(environment) + 를(object marker) + 고려하여(considering-and) + 새(new) + 정책(policy) + 를(object marker) + 수립해야(should establish) + 한다(do)
examples.english: We must establish a new policy considering the environment.

rules.title: Evaluative Context
rules.description: When used with -할 때, it frames a conditional evaluation of suitability or appropriateness.

examples.korean: 학생들의 수준을 고려할 때 이 교재가 적합하다.
examples.breakdown: 학생들의(students') + 수준(level) + 을(object marker) + 고려할 때(when considering) + 이(this) + 교재(textbook) + 가(subject marker) + 적합하다(is suitable)
examples.english: This textbook is suitable when considering the students' level.

notes.title: Formal Register
notes.text: 고려하다 is distinctly more formal than everyday alternatives like 생각하다. Reserve it for reports, academic papers, proposals, and official documents.

notes.title: Conjunctive Forms
notes.text: The connective forms 고려하여 and 고려해서 are extremely common in written Korean, linking the consideration to a subsequent action or policy.

notes.title: Related Expressions
notes.text: 참작하다 (take into consideration with leniency) and 숙고하다 (ponder deeply) occupy nearby semantic territory but carry slightly different nuances.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'to take into account' or 'to make allowances for.' While similar to 고려하다, 감안하다 carries a stronger nuance of adjusting one's judgment after factoring in a specific circumstance, often implying leniency or recalibration.

rules.title: Making Allowances
rules.description: Use 감안하다 when evaluating a situation that requires adjusting standards or expectations based on specific conditions.

examples.korean: 날씨를 감안하면 오늘 야외 공연은 무리다.
examples.breakdown: 날씨(weather) + 를(object marker) + 감안하면(if taking into account) + 오늘(today) + 야외(outdoor) + 공연(performance) + 는(topic marker) + 무리다(is excessive)
examples.english: Taking the weather into account, an outdoor performance today is too much.

rules.title: Adjusted Judgment
rules.description: Often used in legal, evaluative, or administrative contexts where a decision is modified because of mitigating factors.

examples.korean: 그의 경제적 상황을 감안해 형을 감형했다.
examples.breakdown: 그의(his) + 경제적(economic) + 상황(situation) + 을(object marker) + 감안해(taking into account-and) + 형(sentence) + 를(object marker) + 감형했다(reduced)
examples.english: Taking his economic situation into account, the sentence was reduced.

notes.title: Adjustment Nuance
notes.text: 감안하다 carries a stronger nuance of adjusting a judgment or making allowances for a specific factor than 고려하다, which is more neutral.

notes.title: Formal Register
notes.text: This expression is rarely used in casual conversation; reserve it for formal evaluations, legal contexts, and academic assessment.

notes.title: Common Structures
notes.text: Frequently appears with -면/으면 (감안하면, 감안할 때) and as an adverbial connective 감안하여.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'based on' or 'with something as a foundation.' This pattern is essential in academic and analytical writing to indicate the fundamental material, experience, or data from which a conclusion, work, or action is derived.

rules.title: Foundational Basis
rules.description: Attach 를/을 to a noun and follow with 바탕으로 to indicate that the noun serves as the foundation. A following verb such as 하다, 삼다, or 만들다 is usually required.

examples.korean: 이 소설은 작가의 실제 경험을 바탕으로 쓰여졌다.
examples.breakdown: 이(this) + 소설(novel) + 은(topic marker) + 작가의(author's) + 실제(actual) + 경험(experience) + 을(object marker) + 바탕으로(as a basis) + 쓰여졌다(was written)
examples.english: This novel was written based on the author's actual experiences.

rules.title: With 삼다
rules.description: The collocation 바탕으로 삼다 explicitly means 'to take as a foundation,' adding a sense of deliberate selection.

examples.korean: 전통 기법을 바탕으로 삼아 현대적인 작품을 만들었다.
examples.breakdown: 전통(traditional) + 기법(technique) + 을(object marker) + 바탕으로 삼아(taking as a basis-and) + 현대적인(modern) + 작품(work) + 을(object marker) + 만들었다(created)
examples.english: Taking traditional techniques as a basis, he created a modern work.

notes.title: Requires a Following Verb
notes.text: 바탕으로 is not a complete predicate; it must combine with 하다, 삼다, 만들다, or a similar verb to form a complete sentence.

notes.title: Core Meaning
notes.text: 바탕 literally means 'foundation, base, or background.' It emphasizes the raw material or fundamental source rather than a mere reference.

notes.title: Common Collocations
notes.text: 경험을 바탕으로, 사실을 바탕으로, 연구를 바탕으로, 데이터를 바탕으로 are among the most frequent pairings in academic Korean.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'on the basis of.' Slightly more formal and academic than 바탕으로, 토대로 is heavily favored in research papers, official reports, and analytical discourse to denote a theoretical or practical foundation.

rules.title: Academic Usage
rules.description: Use 토대로 to indicate that research, analysis, or a project is founded upon specific data, theory, or records.

examples.korean: 이 논문은 설문 조사 결과를 토대로 분석되었다.
examples.breakdown: 이(this) + 논문(paper) + 은(topic marker) + 설문 조사(survey) + 결과(results) + 를(object marker) + 토대로(as a basis) + 분석되었다(was analyzed)
examples.english: This paper was analyzed on the basis of survey results.

rules.title: With 삼다
rules.description: 토대로 삼다 explicitly states that something is adopted as the foundational principle or material for further work.

examples.korean: 역사적 기록을 토대로 삼아 드라마를 제작했다.
examples.breakdown: 역사적(historical) + 기록(records) + 을(object marker) + 토대로 삼아(taking as a basis-and) + 드라마(drama) + 를(object marker) + 제작했다(produced)
examples.english: Taking historical records as a basis, they produced the drama.

notes.title: Higher Register
notes.text: 토대로 is slightly more academic and formal than 바탕으로, though they are often interchangeable in practice.

notes.title: Sino-Korean Origin
notes.text: 토대(土臺) literally means 'earthen platform or foundation,' giving it a more technical and constructed feel than the native 바탕.

notes.title: Common Pairings
notes.text: 조사를 토대로, 이론을 토대로, 자료를 토대로, and 사실을 토대로 are staples of formal research writing.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'on the premise of' or 'assuming as a precondition.' This pattern is fundamental in logic, law, and contract language where a proposition or action depends on a prior condition being accepted as true.

rules.title: Logical Premise
rules.description: Use 전제로 to state that an agreement, plan, or argument rests upon a specific assumption or precondition.

examples.breakdown: 양측의(both sides') + 협조(cooperation) + 를(object marker) + 전제로(as a premise) + 계획(plan) + 을(object marker) + 세웠다(drew up)
examples.english: The plan was drawn up on the premise of cooperation from both sides.

rules.title: Modifier Form
rules.description: In its modifier form 전제로 한, it can directly modify a following noun, creating adjectival phrases common in contracts and legal texts.

examples.korean: 그의 무죄를 전제로 한 영화는 논란이 되었다.
examples.breakdown: 그의(his) + 무죄(innocence) + 를(object marker) + 전제로 한(based on the premise of) + 영화(movie) + 는(topic marker) + 논란이 되었다(became controversial)
examples.english: The movie based on the premise of his innocence became controversial.

notes.title: Logical Dependency
notes.text: 전제로 implies a logical or contractual dependency; if the premise fails, the main proposition or plan typically collapses.

notes.title: Formal Contexts
notes.text: Essential in legal documents, philosophical arguments, business contracts, and diplomatic negotiations.

notes.title: Common Structure
notes.text: Often appears as -을/를 전제로 하다 or -을/를 전제로 한 + noun in written discourse.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'on the condition of.' This pattern is indispensable in negotiations, contracts, and agreements where one party sets a specific requirement that must be met for an action or promise to hold.

rules.title: Setting Conditions
rules.description: Use 조건으로 to present a requirement or stipulation that governs a subsequent promise, action, or agreement.

examples.korean: 정부는 핵 포기를 조건으로 경제 지원을 약속했다.
examples.breakdown: 정부는(the government) + 핵(nuclear) + 포기(renunciation) + 를(object marker) + 조건으로(as a condition) + 경제(economic) + 지원(support) + 을(object marker) + 약속했다(promised)
examples.english: The government promised economic support on the condition of nuclear renunciation.

rules.title: Conditional Clause
rules.description: The form 조건으로 하여 connects the condition to the main clause with a formal, written nuance.

examples.korean: 성적 향상을 조건으로 하여 장학금을 지급한다.
examples.breakdown: 성적(grades) + 향상(improvement) + 을(object marker) + 조건으로 하여(making a condition-and) + 장학금(scholarship) + 을(object marker) + 지급한다(provide)
examples.english: Scholarships are provided on the condition of grade improvement.

notes.title: Negotiation Language
notes.text: 조건으로 is fundamental in diplomacy, contracts, bargaining, and any formal setting where terms must be explicitly stated.

notes.title: Requires a Following Action
notes.text: Like other -으로 structures, this requires a main verb such as 약속하다, 제시하다, 내걸다, or 하다 to complete the sentence.

notes.title: Related Pattern
notes.text: -을/를 조건으로 (하면/삼으면) is also used to form extended conditional clauses in complex sentences.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'by means of' or 'using as a tool.' This pattern often carries an analytical or critical tone, especially when discussing how people, objects, or policies are instrumentalized to achieve a goal.

rules.title: Means to an End
rules.description: Use 수단으로 to state that something is employed as an instrument or method for achieving a larger objective.

examples.korean: 그는 돈을 수단으로 삼아 권력을 추구했다.
examples.breakdown: 그는(he) + 돈(money) + 을(object marker) + 수단으로 삼아(taking as a means-and) + 권력(power) + 을(object marker) + 추구했다(pursued)
examples.english: He pursued power by taking money as his means.

rules.title: Analytical Usage
rules.description: In policy or social analysis, 수단으로 describes the mechanism through which an organization or government attempts to reach a target.

examples.korean: 이 정책은 규제 완화를 수단으로 경제 활성화를 꾀하고 있다.
examples.breakdown: 이(this) + 정책(policy) + 은(topic marker) + 규제 완화(deregulation) + 를(object marker) + 수단으로(as a means) + 경제 활성화(economic revitalization) + 를(object marker) + 꾀하고 있다(is aiming for)
examples.english: This policy is aiming for economic revitalization by means of deregulation.

notes.title: Negative Connotation
notes.text: 수단으로 often implies instrumentalization, especially when people are treated as tools: 사람을 수단으로 삼지 말라 (Never treat people merely as means).

notes.title: Formal and Analytical
notes.text: Common in sociology, politics, and philosophy where means-end relationships are critically examined.

notes.title: Philosophical Resonance
notes.text: Kant's categorical imperative 'never merely as a means' translates directly using 수단으로 삼다.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'by way of' or 'using as a method.' More neutral and procedural than 수단으로, this pattern describes the selection of a particular procedure, approach, or methodology.

rules.title: Method Selection
rules.description: Use 방법으로 to indicate that a particular course of action has been chosen as the procedure for accomplishing something.

examples.breakdown: 협상(negotiation) + 을(object marker) + 방법으로(as a method) + 갈등(conflict) + 을(object marker) + 해결하려(resolve-try to) + 했다(did)
examples.english: They tried to resolve the conflict by way of negotiation.

rules.title: With 택하다
rules.description: The collocation 방법으로 택하다 explicitly means 'to choose as the method,' highlighting deliberate selection among alternatives.

examples.breakdown: 대화(dialogue) + 를(object marker) + 방법으로(as a method) + 택하는(choosing) + 것이(thing-subject) + 현명하다(is wise)
examples.english: Choosing dialogue as the method is wise.

notes.title: Neutral Tone
notes.text: 방법으로 is neutral and procedural, whereas 수단으로 can sound calculating or instrumental. Use 방법으로 for straightforward descriptions of process.

notes.title: Common Verbs
notes.text: 방법으로 pairs naturally with 택하다, 삼다, 하다, and 모색하다 (seek) in formal writing.

notes.title: Usage Contexts
notes.text: Frequently found in instructional manuals, policy documents, scientific methodology sections, and procedural descriptions.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'on the grounds of' or 'by reason of.' This is an extremely formal, bureaucratic expression used almost exclusively in legal, administrative, and official documents to state the justification for an action.

rules.title: Official Justification
rules.description: Use 이유로 to present the formal grounds for a resignation, dismissal, ruling, or official decision.

examples.breakdown: 그는(he) + 건강상의(health-related) + 문제(problem) + 를(object marker) + 이유로(as a reason) + 사임했다(resigned)
examples.english: He resigned on the grounds of health problems.

rules.title: Legal Usage
rules.description: In legal contexts, 이유로 is the standard phrasing for explaining why a case was dismissed, rejected, or approved.

examples.breakdown: 증거 불충분(insufficient evidence) + 을(object marker) + 이유로(as a reason) + 재판(trial) + 이(subject marker) + 기각되었다(was dismissed)
examples.english: The trial was dismissed on the grounds of insufficient evidence.

notes.title: Very Formal Register
notes.text: 이유로 is primarily used in legal, administrative, and official documents. It sounds stiff and bureaucratic in everyday speech.

notes.title: Not for Casual Conversation
notes.text: In everyday speech, use -아/어서 or -(으)니까 to give reasons. 이유로 belongs to the written and official register.

notes.title: Variant Form
notes.text: 이유로서 is an even more formal written variant meaning 'by reason of,' often encountered in older legal texts.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'under the pretext of' or 'using as a nominal justification.' This pattern reveals a gap between the stated reason and the true motive, and is heavily used in critical analysis, journalism, and exposés.

rules.title: Socially Acceptable Pretext
rules.description: Use 명분으로 when a party uses a nominally justifiable reason to mask or legitimize an underlying agenda.

examples.breakdown: 정부는(the government) + 안보(security) + 를(object marker) + 명분으로(as a pretext) + 언론(press) + 을(object marker) + 통제했다(controlled)
examples.english: The government controlled the press under the pretext of security.

rules.title: Exposing True Motives
rules.description: The pattern frequently appears in contexts where the speaker aims to uncover the real intention behind an outwardly reasonable action.

examples.korean: 그는 회식을 명분으로 친구들과 술자리를 가졌다.
examples.breakdown: 그는(he) + 회식(company dinner) + 을(object marker) + 명분으로(as a pretext) + 친구들과(with friends) + 술자리(drinking gathering) + 를(object marker) + 가졌다(had)
examples.english: He had a drinking gathering with friends under the pretext of a company dinner.

notes.title: Critical Tone
notes.text: 명분으로 almost always carries the nuance that the stated reason is not the true motive; it exposes the gap between appearance and reality.

notes.title: Journalistic Favorite
notes.text: Extremely common in news analysis, editorials, and social commentary where power dynamics and hidden agendas are discussed.

notes.title: Etymology
notes.text: 명분(名分) combines 'name' (名) and 'duty' (分), meaning a nominally justifiable reason that satisfies social expectations.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'under the excuse of.' Less formal than 명분으로 but still common in advanced discourse, this pattern is used when someone uses a fabricated or minor reason to avoid an obligation or justify self-serving behavior.

rules.title: Common Excuse
rules.description: Use 핑계로 to describe everyday situations where someone uses a pretext to avoid responsibility or gain personal advantage.

examples.korean: 애들은 감기를 핑계로 학교에 가기 싫어했다.
examples.breakdown: 애들은(the kids) + 감기(cold) + 를(object marker) + 핑계로(as an excuse) + 학교(school) + 에(to) + 가기(going) + 싫어했다(disliked)
examples.english: The kids didn't want to go to school using a cold as an excuse.

rules.title: With Modifying Clause
rules.description: The structure -다는 핑계로 is extremely common, where the modifier clause provides the specific content of the excuse.

examples.breakdown: 그는(he) + 바쁘다는(being busy-said) + 핑계로(as an excuse) + 약속(appointment) + 을(object marker) + 피했다(avoided)
examples.english: He avoided the appointment under the excuse of being busy.

notes.title: More Colloquial
notes.text: 핑계로 is less formal than 명분으로 and can be used in everyday contexts, though it still belongs to the advanced register when analyzed critically.

notes.title: Common Structure
notes.text: -라는/다는 핑계로 is one of the most frequent constructions, allowing full clauses to serve as the content of the excuse.

notes.title: Related Expressions
notes.text: 구실로 (even more colloquial) and 변명으로 (apology/excuse) are near-synonyms with slightly different registers and connotations.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'starting from' or 'with something as the dividing line.' This pattern creates a clear before-and-after boundary, whether temporal, spatial, or conceptual, and is essential in historical, geographical, and analytical writing.

rules.title: Temporal Dividing Line
rules.description: Use 기점으로 to mark a specific point in time after which a significant change or trend began.

examples.breakdown: 1987년(1987 year) + 을(object marker) + 기점으로(as a starting point) + 한국의(Korea's) + 민주화(democratization) + 가(subject marker) + 본격화되었다(gained momentum)
examples.english: Democratization in Korea gained momentum starting from 1987.

rules.title: Spatial Boundary
rules.description: 기점으로 can also mark a physical boundary where conditions, cultures, or jurisdictions change.

examples.korean: 이 강을 기점으로 두 지역의 문화가 달라진다.
examples.breakdown: 이(this) + 강(river) + 을(object marker) + 기점으로(as a starting point) + 두(two) + 지역의(regions') + 문화(culture) + 가(subject marker) + 달라진다(differs)
examples.english: The cultures of the two regions differ starting from this river.

notes.title: Clear Before/After
notes.text: 기점으로 implies a distinct boundary line, creating an unambiguous 'before and after' or 'here and there' division.

notes.title: Temporal vs Spatial
notes.text: While most commonly used with time expressions, it can also mark spatial boundaries in geographical and urban planning descriptions.

notes.title: Distinction from 계기로
notes.text: -을/를 계기로 focuses on causal opportunity (a catalyst), while 기점으로 focuses on a dividing line (a baseline).

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'as an opportunity' or 'as a catalyst.' This pattern is essential in reflective, historical, and autobiographical writing to indicate that an event served as the trigger for subsequent change or action. The original text uses the hanja form 契機, but the standard Korean writing is 계기로.

rules.title: Turning Point
rules.description: Use 계기로 to describe a pivotal moment or experience that caused a person, organization, or society to change direction.

examples.korean: 유학 생활을 계기로 그는 완전히 달라졌다.
examples.breakdown: 유학(study abroad) + 생활(life) + 을(object marker) + 계기로(as an opportunity) + 그는(he) + 완전히(completely) + 달라졌다(changed)
examples.english: He changed completely taking his study abroad life as an opportunity.

rules.title: With 삼다
rules.description: The collocation 계기로 삼다 explicitly means 'to take as an opportunity,' emphasizing a deliberate decision to use an event as motivation.

examples.korean: 이번 대회를 계기로 삼아 열심히 연습하기로 했다.
examples.breakdown: 이번(this) + 대회(competition) + 를(object marker) + 계기로 삼아(taking as an opportunity-and) + 열심히(diligently) + 연습(practice) + 하기로(deciding to) + 했다(did)
examples.english: I decided to practice diligently taking this competition as an opportunity.

notes.title: Hanja Note
notes.text: 계기(契機) consists of 契 (contract/pledge) and 機 (opportunity/machine), meaning a pivotal moment that triggers change. The standard Korean orthography is 계기로.

notes.title: Catalyst for Change
notes.text: 계기로 always implies that the noun becomes a catalyst for subsequent transformation, learning, or decisive action.

notes.title: Common Collocations
notes.text: ~를 계기로 is one of the most common expressions in autobiographical writing, historical accounts, and reflective essays.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'on the surface' or 'using as the outward appearance.' This pattern almost always implies a sharp contrast between what is presented externally and what exists internally or in reality.

rules.title: Outward Appearance
rules.description: Use 표면으로 to describe a public facade or official stance that conceals a different underlying reality.

examples.korean: 회사는 친환경을 표면으로 내세우지만 실제로는 그렇지 않다.
examples.breakdown: 회사는(the company) + 친환경(environmental friendliness) + 을(object marker) + 표면으로(as a surface) + 내세우지만(promotes-but) + 실제로는(in reality) + 그렇지 않다(is not so)
examples.english: The company promotes environmental friendliness on the surface, but in reality it is not so.

rules.title: Modifier Form
rules.description: The form 표면으로 한 can modify a noun, creating adjectival phrases that describe relationships or actions based on a facade.

examples.korean: 그들은 우정을 표면으로 한 경쟁 관계를 유지했다.
examples.breakdown: 그들은(they) + 우정(friendship) + 을(object marker) + 표면으로 한(using as a surface) + 경쟁(competition) + 관계(relationship) + 를(object marker) + 유지했다(maintained)
examples.english: They maintained a competitive relationship using friendship as the surface.

notes.title: Implies Deception
notes.text: 표면으로 almost always signals that the outward appearance contrasts with a hidden inner reality, often with a critical or skeptical tone.

notes.title: Critical Tone
notes.text: Common in investigative journalism, exposés, and critical social analysis where discrepancies between image and reality are examined.

notes.title: Common Verbs
notes.text: 표면으로 pairs with 내세우다 (promote), 삼다 (take as), 하다 (do), and 드러내다 (reveal) in formal writing.

explanation: Attaches to a noun to mean 'as reality' or 'treating as the real thing.' This advanced pattern is used to contrast abstract concepts, virtual states, or theories with concrete reality. It is considerably less common than other -으로 patterns and usually requires verbs like 삼다 or 여기다.

rules.title: Treating as Reality
rules.description: Use 실제로 삼다 to indicate that something abstract or virtual is being treated or lived as if it were real.

examples.korean: 게임 속 세계를 실제로 삼아 사는 청소년들이 늘고 있다.
examples.breakdown: 게임(game) + 속(inside) + 세계(world) + 를(object marker) + 실제로 삼아(taking as reality-and) + 사는(living) + 청소년들이(youths) + 늘고 있다(are increasing)
examples.english: Youths who live taking the in-game world as reality are increasing.

rules.title: Considering as Real
rules.description: Use 실제로 여기다 to express that someone regards or considers a concept, image, or representation as the real thing.

examples.breakdown: 가상현실(virtual reality) + 을(object marker) + 실제로 여기는(considering as reality) + 현상(phenomenon) + 이(subject marker) + 심각하다(is serious)
examples.english: The phenomenon of considering virtual reality as actual reality is serious.

notes.title: Rare Pattern
notes.text: N-을/를 실제로 is considerably less common than other -으로 patterns and almost always requires 삼다, 여기다, or 보다 to complete the meaning.

notes.title: Reality vs Theory
notes.text: Used to contrast abstract concepts with concrete reality, often in philosophical, psychological, and media studies contexts.

notes.title: Adverbial Confusion
notes.text: Be careful not to confuse this with 실제로 as a sentence adverb meaning 'actually' (e.g., 실제로 그는 왔다 = Actually, he came).

explanation: This grammar is used in formal writing, reports, and academic contexts to present a logical conclusion or result based on a preceding cause or reason. It carries a more objective and official tone than casual spoken connectives like -아/어서 or -니까.

rules.title: Stems ending in a consonant (받침)
rules.description: Attach -(으)므로 to verb or adjective stems that end in a consonant.

examples.english: The train was delayed, therefore I could not attend the meeting.

examples.korean: 비용이 많이 들었으므로 계획을 수정해야 한다.
examples.english: The costs have risen considerably, so the plan must be revised.

rules.title: Stems ending in a vowel (no 받침)
rules.description: Attach -므로 directly to verb or adjective stems that end in a vowel.

examples.english: The weather is favorable, therefore we will proceed with the outdoor event.
His opinion is valid, so we decided to accept it.

rules.title: Irregular ㄹ stems
rules.description: For stems ending in ㄹ, drop the ㄹ before attaching -므로.

examples.korean: 자료가 부족하므로 조사를 더 해야 한다.
examples.english: The data is insufficient, therefore further investigation is needed.

notes.title: Formal written register
notes.text: Used almost exclusively in formal written Korean. Avoid in casual conversation.

notes.title: Common contexts
notes.text: Often appears in official documents, news articles, academic papers, and formal announcements.

notes.title: Result clause endings
notes.text: The result clause typically uses formal endings such as -습니다, -ㅂ니다, -아/어야 한다, or -이다.

explanation: A formal connective that indicates a reason or cause, similar to -아/어서 or -(으)니까, but with a more elevated and objective tone. It is commonly found in written discourse and formal speech where neutrality is preferred.

rules.title: All verb and adjective stems
rules.description: Attach -기에 directly to any verb or adjective stem without any 받침 adjustments.

examples.english: Time is tight, so a quick decision is necessary.

examples.korean: 그의 실력이 뛰어나기에 팀의 핵심 멤버로 선발되었다.
examples.english: His abilities are outstanding, so he was selected as a core team member.

rules.title: With tense markers
rules.description: Past tense -았/었- or progressive -고 있- can precede 기에 to add temporal nuance.

examples.korean: 그 일을 이미 알고 있기에 놀라지 않았다.
examples.english: I was not surprised because I already knew about that matter.

examples.korean: 경제가 어려웠기에 많은 기업이 어려움을 겪었다.
examples.english: The economy was difficult, so many companies suffered hardships.

notes.title: Objective tone
notes.text: 기에 sounds more objective and less personal than -(으)니까, making it ideal for formal analysis.

notes.title: Explanatory usage
notes.text: Frequently used in explanatory or evaluative statements in essays, reports, and official evaluations.

notes.title: Not for giving commands
notes.text: Avoid using 기에 when the result clause is a direct command or strong suggestion; use -(으)니까 instead.

exceptions.name: ㅂ irregular adjectives
exceptions.description: Adjectives ending in ㅂ change to 우 before vowel-initial endings, affecting the stem before past tense.

explanation: Used in formal and legal contexts to indicate that an action is based on a foundation, document, law, or standard. It elevates the tone significantly and signals institutional or procedural legitimacy.

rules.title: Noun + 에 의거하여 (based on)
rules.description: Attach -에 의거하여 to a noun to show that something is grounded in or derived from it.

examples.korean: 회사 규정에 의거하여 징계 위원회를 소집합니다.
examples.english: Based on company regulations, we are convening a disciplinary committee.
Evidence was collected in accordance with the relevant laws.

rules.title: Noun + 에 의하여 (by means of / due to)
rules.description: Attach -에 의하여 to a noun to indicate causation, means, or agency, often in passive constructions.

examples.korean: 그의 노력에 의하여 프로젝트가 성공적으로 마무리되었다.
examples.english: The project was successfully completed thanks to his efforts.

examples.korean: 외부 요인에 의하여 시스템에 오류가 발생했다.
examples.english: An error occurred in the system due to external factors.

notes.title: 의거하여 vs 의하여
notes.text: 에 의거하여 specifically means based on and is used with laws, rules, or data. 에 의하여 indicates causation or means, often in passive constructions.

notes.title: Formal register only
notes.text: These forms are very formal and rarely used in everyday conversation. Use -에 따라서 or -때문에 for casual contexts.

notes.title: Common collocations
notes.text: Frequently paired with nouns like 법률, 규정, 계약, 조약, 데이터, and 조사.

explanation: Expresses that an action is carried out through a medium, channel, person, or process. It is widely used in both formal writing and everyday Korean to highlight the pathway or means by which something is achieved.

rules.title: Noun + 을 통하여 / 통해 (받침 O)
rules.description: Attach -을 통하여 or -을 통해 to nouns ending in a consonant.

examples.korean: 이 프로그램은 교육을 통하여 학생들의 창의력을 향상시킨다.
examples.english: This program enhances students creativity through education.

examples.korean: 온라인 플랫폼을 통해 제품을 판매하고 있다.
examples.english: They are selling products through an online platform.

rules.title: Noun + 를 통하여 / 통해 (받침 X)
rules.description: Attach -를 통하여 or -를 통해 to nouns ending in a vowel.

examples.korean: 현지 통역사를 통해 중요한 정보를 얻었다.
examples.english: I obtained important information through a local interpreter.

examples.korean: 다양한 경험을 통하여 인생의 지혜를 얻는다.
examples.english: One gains the wisdom of life through diverse experiences.

notes.title: 통하여 vs 통해
notes.text: 통하여 and 통해 are interchangeable in meaning, but 통해 is more commonly used in spoken language while 통하여 is more formal.

notes.title: Abstract and physical channels
notes.text: Can indicate both physical channels like people or institutions and abstract ones like media, experiences, or processes.

notes.title: Passive pairings
notes.text: Often paired with passive verbs like 전달되다, 알려지다, 이루어지다, or 달성되다.

explanation: A connective that adds one fact on top of another, emphasizing that both are true. It functions like not only but also and often pairs with a following clause that reinforces or intensifies the situation.

rules.title: Adjective stem + -(으)ㄴ 데다(가)
rules.description: For adjectives, use -(으)ㄴ 데다(가). Add 은 after consonant stems, ㄴ after vowel stems.

examples.korean: 그 도시는 물가가 비싼 데다 교통도 복잡하다.
examples.english: That city not only has high prices but also has complicated traffic.

examples.korean: 그 문제는 어려운 데다 시간도 많이 걸린다.
examples.english: That problem is not only difficult but also takes a lot of time.

rules.title: Verb stem + -는 데다(가)
rules.description: For verbs, attach -는 데다(가) directly to the stem.

examples.korean: 그는 술을 마시는 데다가 답변까지 핀다.
examples.english: He not only drinks alcohol but also smokes.

examples.korean: 직장이 멀리 있는 데다 교통비도 비싸서 힘들다.
examples.english: My workplace is not only far away but the commute is also expensive, so it is tough.

notes.title: Additive nuance
notes.text: The second clause usually contains an additional fact that reinforces or intensifies the first, often negative.

notes.title: 데다가 variant
notes.text: 데다가 is a slightly more emphatic spoken variant of 데다. Both are grammatically correct and interchangeable.

notes.title: Avoid confusion with 데에
notes.text: Do not confuse with -(으)ㄴ/는 데에, which marks a place or point in time. Context distinguishes them clearly.

notes.title: Emotional tone
notes.text: Commonly used when listing two unfavorable facts, though it can be neutral depending on context.

exceptions.name: ㅂ irregular adjectives
exceptions.description: Adjectives ending in ㅂ change to 우 before a vowel, so 춥다 becomes 추운 데다.

explanation: Used to pinpoint the core issue, problem, or significance of a matter. It literally means it lies in the fact that and is essential in analytical writing and discussion where identifying the crux of an argument is required.

rules.title: Adjective stem + -(으)ㄴ 데 있다
rules.description: For adjectives, use -(으)ㄴ 데 있다. Add 은 after consonant stems, ㄴ after vowel stems.

examples.korean: 이 정책의 문제점은 실행이 늦은 데 있다.
examples.breakdown: 늦은 데 있다 = 늦 (stem) + 은 데 있다
examples.english: The problem with this policy lies in the fact that its implementation is delayed.

examples.korean: 이 사태의 심각성은 대응이 미흡한 데 있다.
examples.breakdown: 미흡한 데 있다 = 미흡하 (stem) + ㄴ 데 있다
examples.english: The seriousness of this situation lies in the inadequate response.

rules.title: Verb stem + -는 데 있다
rules.description: For verbs, attach -는 데 있다 directly to the stem.

examples.korean: 우리의 목표는 단순히 이기는 데 있는 것이 아니라 과정을 중시하는 데 있다.
examples.breakdown: 이기는 데 있다 = 이기 (stem) + 는 데 있다
examples.english: Our goal lies not merely in winning but in valuing the process.

examples.korean: 그 책의 가치는 역사를 새롭게 해석하는 데 있다.
examples.breakdown: 해석하는 데 있다 = 해석하 (stem) + 는 데 있다
examples.english: The value of that book lies in its new interpretation of history.

notes.title: Academic register
notes.text: This structure is common in thesis abstracts, critiques, analytical essays, and formal evaluations.

notes.title: Subject of analysis
notes.text: The noun before 에 often defines the subject being analyzed, such as 문제점, 의의, 가치, or 목적.

notes.title: Ambiguity with locational 데
notes.text: Do not confuse with -는 데 있다 meaning exists in a place. Context makes the difference clear.

explanation: Used to limit the scope of discussion to a particular field, aspect, or domain. It is the formal equivalent of in terms of or with regard to, and is indispensable in comparative and analytical writing.

rules.title: Noun + -에 있어서
rules.description: Attach -에 있어서 directly to a noun to specify the domain under discussion.

examples.korean: 경제적 측면에 있어서 이 프로젝트는 매우 효율적이다.
examples.english: In terms of economic aspects, this project is very efficient.

examples.korean: 안전성에 있어서는 어떤 제품도 완벽하지 않다.
examples.breakdown: 안전성에 있어서는 = 안전성 (noun) + 에 있어서 + 는 (topic)
examples.english: In terms of safety, no product is perfect.

rules.title: Verb stem + -는 데 있어서
rules.description: When referring to an action, attach -는 데 있어서 to the verb stem.

examples.korean: 학생들을 가르치는 데 있어서 인내심이 가장 중요하다.
examples.breakdown: 가르치는 데 있어서 = 가르치 (stem) + 는 데 있어서
examples.english: In teaching students, patience is the most important thing.

examples.korean: 의사결정을 내리는 데 있어서 객관적 자료가 필수적이다.
examples.breakdown: 내리는 데 있어서 = 내리 (stem) + 는 데 있어서
examples.english: In making decisions, objective data is essential.

notes.title: Topic marker 는
notes.text: Adding 는 after 에 있어서 highlights or contrasts the specific area being discussed.

notes.title: Abstract noun pairings
notes.text: Can be used with abstract nouns like 관계, 측면, 분야, 점, or 영역.

notes.title: Not for physical locations
notes.text: Do not use 에 있어서 for literal physical places. Use -에서 instead.

notes.title: Evaluative adjectives
notes.text: Frequently paired with evaluative adjectives like 중요하다, 필수적이다, 유리하다, or 효과적이다.

explanation: These noun-modifying forms express the topic or target of attention, research, concern, or discussion. They are fundamental in academic and formal writing for introducing subjects and specifying focus areas.

rules.title: Noun + -에 관한 (about / concerning)
rules.description: Attach -에 관한 to a noun to create a modifier meaning about or concerning. It is slightly more objective and formal.

examples.korean: 환경 보호에 관한 국제 회의가 다음 달에 열릴 예정이다.
examples.english: An international conference concerning environmental protection is scheduled for next month.

examples.korean: 그는 역사에 관한 다양한 논문을 발표했다.
examples.english: He presented various papers on history.

rules.title: Noun + -에 대한 (toward / about / regarding)
rules.description: Attach -에 대한 to a noun to create a modifier meaning regarding or toward. It is more versatile and can express emotional attitudes.

examples.korean: 정부의 정책에 대한 시민들의 반응이 뜨겁다.
examples.english: Citizens reactions to the governments policy are heated.

examples.korean: 이 보고서는 교육 개혁에 대한 구체적인 방안을 제시한다.
examples.english: This report presents concrete plans regarding educational reform.

notes.title: 관한 vs 대한 nuance
notes.text: 에 관한 is slightly more objective and formal, often used with research, reports, or studies. 에 대한 is more versatile and can express emotional attitudes like 반응, 관심, or 불만.

notes.title: Must modify a noun
notes.text: Both must modify a following noun. They cannot end a sentence alone.

notes.title: Avoid 관하여 / 대하여 as modifiers
notes.text: Common error: using 에 관하여 or 에 대하여 as noun modifiers instead of 에 관한 or 에 대한.

explanation: A highly formal and somewhat literary connective expressing that since a certain condition or fact is already established, a subsequent action or state naturally follows. It carries the nuance of now that or since, often with a sense of resignation or acceptance.

rules.title: Stems ending in a consonant (받침)
rules.description: Attach -(으)ㄹ진대 to verb or adjective stems that end in a consonant.

examples.korean: 서로의 입장이 이렇게 달라질진대 협상이 쉽지 않을 것이다.
examples.english: Now that our positions have diverged so much, negotiations will not be easy.
Since a promise has been made, it must be kept.

rules.title: Stems ending in a vowel (no 받침)
rules.description: Attach -ㄹ진대 directly to verb or adjective stems that end in a vowel.

examples.korean: 비가 올진대 우산을 챙기는 것이 좋겠다.
examples.english: Since it looks like rain, it would be good to take an umbrella.
Now that it has come to this, nothing can be done.

notes.title: Literary and formal register
notes.text: This is an advanced, literary form rarely used in modern casual conversation.

notes.title: Sense of inevitability
notes.text: Often conveys a sense of inevitability or acceptance of an established fact.

notes.title: Common genres
notes.text: Commonly found in classical literature, formal speeches, historical dramas, and official proclamations.

notes.title: Following clause patterns
notes.text: The following clause often contains a logical consequence, advice, or expression of resignation.

exceptions.name: ㄹ-irregular stems
exceptions.description: For stems ending in ㄹ, the ㄹ is dropped before attaching -ㄹ진대, leaving just -진대.

explanation: A proportional conditional pattern indicating that as one action or state increases or progresses, another action or state correspondingly increases or changes. It is equivalent to the more... the more and is essential for expressing correlation.

rules.title: Stems ending in a consonant (받침)
rules.description: Attach -(으)ㄹ수록 to verb stems that end in a consonant.

examples.english: The more you take this medicine, the worse the side effects become.
The more I listened to his words, the more suspicious they sounded.

rules.title: Stems ending in a vowel (no 받침)
rules.description: Attach -ㄹ수록 directly to verb stems that end in a vowel.

examples.english: The more you learn Korean, the more interesting it becomes.
The more you go to your hometown, the more affection you feel.

rules.title: With adjectives
rules.description: -(으)ㄹ수록 can also attach to adjective stems to express proportional change in states.

examples.english: The more difficult something is, the more it is worth the challenge.

notes.title: Paired clauses
notes.text: Always used in pairs: the first clause sets the increasing condition, and the second clause shows the resulting change.

notes.title: ㄹ-irregular verbs
notes.text: For ㄹ-ending stems like 알다, 만들다, and 살다, the ㄹ drops before -을수록: 알수록, 만들수록, 살수록.

notes.title: Common collocations
notes.text: Frequently used with 볼수록, 들을수록, 갈수록, 할수록, and 먹을수록.

exceptions.name: ㄹ-irregular verbs
exceptions.description: Verbs ending in ㄹ drop the ㄹ before attaching -을수록.
exceptions.example: 알다 -> 알수록, 만들다 -> 만들수록, 살다 -> 살수록

exceptions.name: ㅂ irregular adjectives
exceptions.description: Adjectives ending in ㅂ change to 우 before a vowel, so 어렵다 becomes 어려울수록.

explanation: A formal additive connective that extends a statement by adding related information. It means not only... but also and is widely used in both writing and formal speech to present multiple supporting facts.

rules.title: Stems ending in a consonant (받침)
rules.description: Attach -(으)ㄹ 뿐 아니라 to verb or adjective stems that end in a consonant.

examples.korean: 이 제품은 품질이 좋을 뿐 아니라 가격도 합리적이다.
examples.breakdown: 좋을 뿐 아니라 = 좋 (stem) + 을 뿐 아니라
examples.english: This product is not only high in quality but also reasonably priced.

examples.korean: 그녀는 영어를 잘할 뿐 아니라 일본어도 유창하다.
examples.breakdown: 잘할 뿐 아니라 = 잘하 (stem) + ㄹ 뿐 아니라
examples.english: She is not only good at English but also fluent in Japanese.

rules.title: Stems ending in a vowel (no 받침)
rules.description: Attach -ㄹ 뿐 아니라 directly to verb or adjective stems that end in a vowel.

examples.korean: 그 도시는 역사적으로 중요할 뿐 아니라 관광 가치도 높다.
examples.breakdown: 중요할 뿐 아니라 = 중요하 (stem) + ㄹ 뿐 아니라
examples.english: That city is not only historically important but also high in tourism value.

examples.korean: 이 법은 국민의 권리를 보장할 뿐 아니라 의묘도 규정한다.
examples.breakdown: 보장할 뿐 아니라 = 보장하 (stem) + ㄹ 뿐 아니라
examples.english: This law not only guarantees citizens rights but also defines their duties.

notes.title: Parallel second clause
notes.text: The second clause is typically parallel in nature and often adds a stronger or complementary point.

notes.title: Formal register
notes.text: More formal than -고 또; commonly used in essays, speeches, presentations, and academic writing.

notes.title: Use -도 in the second clause
notes.text: The verb or noun in the second clause is often followed by -도 to reinforce the additive meaning.

notes.title: Variant form
notes.text: Can be shortened to -(으)ㄹ뿐만 아니라 with the same meaning and slightly more emphasis.

explanation: Used to explicitly reject one idea or expectation and replace it with another. It carries the nuance of correction or clarification, making it essential for argumentative writing and precise expression.

rules.title: Stems ending in a consonant (받침)
rules.description: Attach -(으)ㄹ 것이 아니라 to verb or adjective stems that end in a consonant.

examples.korean: 책을 단순히 읽을 것이 아니라 내용을 깊이 이해해야 한다.
examples.breakdown: 읽을 것이 아니라 = 읽 (stem) + 을 것이 아니라
examples.english: You should not simply read the book but deeply understand its content.

examples.korean: 결정이 늦어질 것이 아니라 신속하게 처리되어야 한다.
examples.breakdown: 늦어질 것이 아니라 = 늦어지 (stem) + ㄹ 것이 아니라
examples.english: The decision should not be delayed but handled swiftly.

rules.title: Stems ending in a vowel (no 받침)
rules.description: Attach -ㄹ 것이 아니라 directly to verb or adjective stems that end in a vowel.

examples.korean: 비난할 것이 아니라 대안을 제시해야 한다.
examples.breakdown: 비난할 것이 아니라 = 비난하 (stem) + ㄹ 것이 아니라
examples.english: Rather than criticizing, we must present alternatives.

examples.korean: 이 문제를 개인의 문제로 볼 것이 아니라 사회적 문제로 봐야 한다.
examples.breakdown: 볼 것이 아니라 = 보 (stem) + ㄹ 것이 아니라
examples.english: This issue should not be seen as a personal problem but as a social one.

notes.title: Correction pattern
notes.text: Always used with a contrasting following clause that presents the correct or preferred alternative.

notes.title: Argumentative writing
notes.text: Frequently appears in persuasive writing, editorials, and logical arguments to reframe a position.

notes.title: Not simple negation
notes.text: Do not confuse with -(으)ㄹ 것이다 negated. This structure is a deliberate correction pattern, not a simple negative prediction.

notes.title: Second clause endings
notes.text: Often paired with -아/어야 한다, -는 것이 낫다, or -는 것이다 in the corrective second clause.

explanation: A contrastive connective used to juxtapose two opposite or differing facts about the same subject or two different subjects. It is the formal equivalent of whereas or while and is essential for comparative analysis.

rules.title: Adjective stem + -(으)ㄴ 반면에
rules.description: For adjectives, use -(으)ㄴ 반면에. Add 은 after consonant stems, ㄴ after vowel stems.

examples.korean: 도시는 편리한 반면에 환경 오염이 심각하다.
examples.english: Cities are convenient, whereas environmental pollution is serious.

examples.korean: 이 지역은 물가가 저렴한 반면에 일자리가 부족하다.
examples.english: This area has low prices, whereas jobs are scarce.

rules.title: Verb stem + -는 반면에
rules.description: For verbs, attach -는 반면에 directly to the stem.

examples.korean: 그는 돈을 많이 버는 반면에 가족과의 시간은 부족하다.
examples.english: He earns a lot of money, whereas he lacks time with his family.

examples.korean: 온라인 수업은 시간이 절약되는 반면에 집중도가 떨어질 수 있다.
examples.english: Online classes save time, whereas concentration may drop.

notes.title: Clear contrast required
notes.text: The two clauses should present a clear contrast or trade-off between two states or actions.

notes.title: Flexible subjects
notes.text: Can compare two different subjects or two opposing aspects of one subject.

notes.title: Academic usage
notes.text: Common in academic writing to present balanced arguments, comparative data, or opposing viewpoints.

exceptions.name: ㄹ-irregular verbs
exceptions.description: For stems ending in ㄹ, the ㄹ drops before -는, so 벌다 becomes 버는.
exceptions.example: 벌다 -> 버는 반면에, 알다 -> 아는 반면에

explanation: Indicates that one action or state is substituted for another. It expresses a clear exchange or replacement relationship, functioning like instead of, and is widely used in both formal and everyday contexts.

rules.title: Adjective stem + -(으)ㄴ 대신에
rules.description: For adjectives, use -(으)ㄴ 대신에. Add 은 after consonant stems, ㄴ after vowel stems.

examples.english: Taxis are expensive, but instead they are fast.

rules.title: Verb stem + -는 대신에
rules.description: For verbs, attach -는 대신에 directly to the stem.

examples.korean: 저녁을 먹는 대신에 욱동을 하기로 했다.
examples.english: I decided to exercise instead of eating dinner.

examples.korean: 휴가를 가는 대신에 보너스를 받기로 했다.
examples.english: I decided to take a bonus instead of going on vacation.

notes.title: Direct substitution
notes.text: Implies a direct substitution. What is given up and what is gained should be logically clear.

notes.title: Casual variant
notes.text: Can be shortened to -는 대신 in casual speech without a change in meaning.

notes.title: Logical exchangeability
notes.text: The two actions should be logically exchangeable within the context of the sentence.

notes.title: Common contexts
notes.text: Often used with agreements, plans, and decisions involving explicit trade-offs.

explanation: Used to present one side of a situation or argument, often implying that there is another side to consider. It corresponds to on the one hand and sets up a multi-faceted discussion in formal writing.

rules.title: Adjective stem + -(으)ㄴ 한편
rules.description: For adjectives, use -(으)ㄴ 한편. Add 은 after consonant stems, ㄴ after vowel stems.

examples.korean: 이 정책은 긍정적인 한편 예상치 못한 부작용도 있다.
examples.english: This policy is positive on the one hand, but it also has unexpected side effects.

examples.korean: 이 지역은 조용한 한편 교통이 불편하다.
examples.english: This area is quiet on the one hand, but transportation is inconvenient.

rules.title: Verb stem + -는 한편
rules.description: For verbs, attach -는 한편 directly to the stem.

examples.korean: 그는 회사를 다니는 한편 대학원에서 공부를 병행하고 있다.
examples.english: On the one hand he works at a company, while on the other he is also pursuing graduate studies.

examples.korean: 정부는 규제를 강화하는 한편 지원 정책도 함께 추진하고 있다.
examples.english: The government is strengthening regulations on one hand while also pushing support policies.

notes.title: Multi-sided view
notes.text: Often paired with another clause introduced by -(으)ㄴ/는 반면에 or another -는 한편 to show both sides.

notes.title: Policy and news analysis
notes.text: Frequently used in policy discussions, academic writing, and news analysis to present balanced perspectives.

notes.title: Not the other hand
notes.text: Does not mean on the other hand by itself. It marks the first perspective in a multi-sided view.

notes.title: 한편으로 variant
notes.text: Can be combined with -으로 to form 한편으로, which functions similarly and is common in formal prose.

explanation: A contrastive marker used to highlight differences between two nouns. It directly compares a subject with another entity and emphasizes that they are not alike in some respect.

rules.title: Noun ending in a vowel + -와는 달리
rules.description: Attach -와는 달리 to nouns that end in a vowel to indicate unlikeness.

examples.korean: 한국은 일본과는 달리 내륙 지역이 넓다.
examples.english: Unlike Japan, Korea has a large inland area.

examples.korean: 이 제품은 이전 모델과는 달리 무게가 훨씬 가볍다.
examples.english: Unlike the previous model, this product is much lighter.

rules.title: Noun ending in a consonant + -과는 달리
rules.description: Attach -과는 달리 to nouns that end in a consonant.

examples.korean: 동생은 형과는 달리 성격이 매우 조용하다.
examples.english: Unlike his older brother, the younger brother has a very quiet personality.

examples.korean: 그 지역은 서울과는 달리 물가가 저렴하다.
examples.english: Unlike Seoul, that area has low prices.

notes.title: Optional 는
notes.text: 는 is optional but commonly included for emphasis or contrast. 와 달리 is also acceptable.

notes.title: Compared noun placement
notes.text: The compared noun is placed immediately before -와/과는 달리, and the main subject follows.

notes.title: Particle rules
notes.text: Use 와 after nouns without 받침 and 과 after nouns with 받침. Do not use 랑/이랑 in formal writing.

notes.title: Descriptive second clause
notes.text: Often paired with descriptive adjectives in the main clause to explain the specific difference.

explanation: Indicates that two nouns share the same characteristic, behavior, or condition. It is the formal equivalent of just like or similarly to and is used to draw parallels in both writing and speech.

rules.title: Noun ending in a vowel + -와 마찬가지로
rules.description: Attach -와 마찬가지로 to nouns that end in a vowel.

examples.english: Just like his older sister, he became a doctor.

examples.korean: 이 식물은 선인장과 마찬가지로 물이 거의 필요 없다.
examples.english: Just like a cactus, this plant hardly needs water.

rules.title: Noun ending in a consonant + -과 마찬가지로
rules.description: Attach -과 마찬가지로 to nouns that end in a consonant.

examples.korean: 이 법은 기존 법률과 마찬가지로 시행될 것이다.
examples.english: Just like existing laws, this law will be enforced.
School should be comfortable just like home.

notes.title: Emphasizing similarity
notes.text: Used to emphasize similarity between two entities in terms of a specific quality or behavior.

notes.title: Literal meaning
notes.text: 마찬가지 means the same thing, so the construction literally means in the same way as.

notes.title: Formal particles only
notes.text: The particle 와/과 follows regular 받침 rules. Do not use 랑/이랑 in formal writing.

notes.title: Academic analogies
notes.text: Often used in academic comparisons, analogies, and parallel arguments.

explanation: Indicates that two or more entities act together or coexist simultaneously. It is used in formal contexts to express cooperation, coexistence, or parallel development, similar to together with or along with.

rules.title: Noun ending in a vowel + -와 더불어
rules.description: Attach -와 더불어 to nouns that end in a vowel.

examples.korean: 정부는 시민 단체와 더불어 환경 캠페인을 전개했다.
examples.english: The government launched an environmental campaign together with civic groups.

examples.korean: 그는 노래와 더불어 춤 실력도 뛰어나다.
examples.english: Along with singing, his dancing skills are also outstanding.

rules.title: Noun ending in a consonant + -과 더불어
rules.description: Attach -과 더불어 to nouns that end in a consonant.

examples.korean: 그녀는 학업과 더불어 사회 봉사 활동도 열심히 하고 있다.
examples.english: She is diligently doing social volunteer work together with her studies.

examples.korean: 기술 발전과 더불어 윤리적 문제도 대두되고 있다.
examples.english: Along with technological development, ethical issues are also emerging.

notes.title: Formal alternative
notes.text: More formal than -하고 같이. Appropriate for news, reports, and official documents.

notes.title: Parallel trends
notes.text: Often used to describe parallel trends, cooperation, or dual achievements occurring simultaneously.

notes.title: Reinforce with -도
notes.text: The main clause usually contains another subject or topic marked with -도 to reinforce the joint nature.

notes.title: Time expressions
notes.text: Can be used with time expressions to mean along with the passage of time.

explanation: A formal additive pattern that extends a statement from one noun to another. It means not only N but also and is frequently used to emphasize the breadth or inclusiveness of a subject in formal discourse.

rules.title: Noun + -뿐 아니라
rules.description: Attach -뿐 아니라 directly to any noun without any copula or particle.

examples.korean: 학생뿐 아니라 교사들도 이 제도에 반대했다.
examples.english: Not only students but also teachers opposed this system.

examples.korean: 이 법은 성인뿐 아니라 미성년자에게도 적용된다.
examples.english: This law applies not only to adults but also to minors.

rules.title: With -도 or -까지 for emphasis
rules.description: The following clause often uses -도 or -까지 to strengthen the additive meaning.

examples.korean: 이 프로젝트는 경제적 이익뿐 아니라 사회적 가치도 창출한다.
examples.english: This project creates not only economic profit but also social value.

examples.korean: 그의 연구는 국낼뿐 아니라 해외에서도 큰 주목을 받았다.
examples.english: His research received great attention not only domestically but also abroad.

notes.title: Equal or greater emphasis
notes.text: The second element is typically of equal or greater importance than the first.

notes.title: TOPIK writing favorite
notes.text: More formal than -뿐만이 아니라. Extremely common in TOPIK writing prompts and formal essays.

notes.title: Direct noun attachment
notes.text: Unlike A/V-(으)ㄹ 뿐 아니라, this pattern attaches directly to the noun without any copula.

notes.title: Avoiding repetition
notes.text: Frequently appears in lists where the writer wants to avoid sounding repetitive.

explanation: Used to dismissively or objectively downgrade the importance, quantity, or degree of a noun, indicating that it is merely or no more than what is stated. It is essential for expressing limitation or understatement in formal analysis.

rules.title: Noun + -에 불과하다
rules.description: Attach -에 불과하다 to a noun to indicate that something is merely or only what the noun describes.

examples.english: His theory is still merely a hypothesis.

examples.korean: 우리가 아는 것은 우주의 극히 일부에 불과하다.
examples.english: What we know is merely a tiny fraction of the universe.

rules.title: Past and future conjugations
rules.description: 불과하다 can be conjugated to past or future forms like 불과했다 or 불과할 것이다.

examples.korean: 그때 그의 나이는 스무 살에 불과했다.
examples.breakdown: 스무 살에 불과했다 = 스무 살 (noun) + 에 불과했다
examples.english: At that time, his age was merely twenty.

examples.korean: 이 보고서는 전체 계획의 시작에 불과할 것이다.
examples.breakdown: 시작에 불과할 것이다 = 시작 (noun) + 에 불과할 것이다
examples.english: This report will be no more than the beginning of the entire plan.

notes.title: Limitation tone
notes.text: Carries a tone of limitation. Often used to put something in perspective by downplaying its scope.

notes.title: Numbers and proportions
notes.text: Commonly used with numbers, proportions, or stages to indicate only or just.

notes.title: Noun only
notes.text: Cannot be used with verbs or adjectives directly. Must nominalize first with -는 것 or -기.

notes.title: Strengthening adverbs
notes.text: Often paired with 아직, 단지, or 겨우 to strengthen the sense of smallness or insufficiency.
